title: podman generate systemd: create directory in /var/run automatically
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I'm running a container that exposes a service via socket in `/var/run/<SERVICE-NAME>` directory.

Let's use the [Intel SGX AESM Service container](https://hub.docker.com/r/fortanix/aesmd/) as an example.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create the container with:

```
sudo podman create \
  --pull always \
  --device /dev/isgx \
  --volume /var/run/aesmd:/var/run/aesmd:Z \
  --name aesmd \
  docker.io/fortanix/aesmd
```

2. Create the `container-aesmd.service` systemd unit file for it:

```
sudo podman generate systemd --restart-policy=always --time 10 --name aesmd | \
  sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/container-aesmd.service
```

For me, this outputs:
```init
# container-aesmd.service
# autogenerated by Podman 3.1.2
# Thu Jul  1 11:38:38 CEST 2021

[Unit]
Description=Podman container-aesmd.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=/var/lib/containers/storage /run/containers/storage

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=always
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman start aesmd
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 aesmd
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 aesmd
PIDFile=/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/a812d157deae70a758292344dd86b1a1ad094c3af6567da14ce319262bca7e82/userdata/conmon.pid
Type=forking

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```

3. Finally, enable and start the `container-aesmd.service` with:

```
sudo systemctl enable container-aesmd.service
sudo systemctl start container-aesmd.service
```

**Describe the results you received:**

After rebooting the system, the `container-aesmd.service` fails with:
```
lstat /var/run/aesmd: no such file or directory
```

Full logs are:
```
Jul 01 11:46:12 toronto systemd[1]: Starting Podman container-aesmd.service...
Jul 01 11:46:12 toronto podman[25183]: Error: unable to start container "a812d157deae70a758292344dd86b1a1ad094c3af6567da14ce319262bca7e82": lstat /var/run/aesmd: no such file or directory
Jul 01 11:46:12 toronto systemd[1]: container-aesmd.service: Control process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
Jul 01 11:46:12 toronto podman[25301]: 2021-07-01 11:46:12.419886597 +0200 CEST m=+0.032344493 container cleanup a812d157deae70a758292344dd86b1a1ad094c3af6567da14ce319262bca7e82 (image=dock>
Jul 01 11:46:12 toronto podman[25301]: aesmd
Jul 01 11:46:12 toronto systemd[1]: container-aesmd.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Jul 01 11:46:12 toronto systemd[1]: Failed to start Podman container-aesmd.service.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The only missing this is adding the following line to the generated systemd unit file (under `[Service]` section).
```
RuntimeDirectory=aesmd
```

The resulting `container-aesmd.service` systemd unit file looks like:
```
# container-aesmd.service
# autogenerated by Podman 3.1.2
# Thu Jul  1 11:52:57 CEST 2021

[Unit]
Description=Podman container-aesmd.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=/var/lib/containers/storage /run/containers/storage

[Service]
RuntimeDirectory=aesmd
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=always
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman start aesmd
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 aesmd
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 aesmd
PIDFile=/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/a812d157deae70a758292344dd86b1a1ad094c3af6567da14ce319262bca7e82/userdata/conmon.pid
Type=forking

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```

After running:

```
sudo systemctl daemon-reload 
sudo systemctl restart container-aesmd.service
```
the `container-aesmd.service` works as expected:

```
Jul 01 11:56:24 toronto systemd[1]: Starting Podman container-aesmd.service...
Jul 01 11:56:24 toronto podman[25410]: 2021-07-01 11:46:24.206963589 +0200 CEST m=+0.142429455 container init a812d157deae70a758292344dd86b1a1ad094c3af6567da14ce319262bca7e82 (image=docker.>
Jul 01 11:56:24 toronto podman[25410]: 2021-07-01 11:46:24.211785928 +0200 CEST m=+0.147251794 container start a812d157deae70a758292344dd86b1a1ad094c3af6567da14ce319262bca7e82 (image=docker>
Jul 01 11:56:24 toronto podman[25410]: aesmd
Jul 01 11:56:24 toronto systemd[1]: Started Podman container-aesmd.service.
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @tjanez!

I am not really sure what the request is, though.  The start of the container fails since the source of the volume isn't present anymore.  I fear there's no general solution that Podman could employ.

Can you elaborate a bit how you wished Podman to behave?
I do not see this as an issue with Podman.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
